The Classic Collection of Immanuel Kant. Illustrated: The Critique Of Pure Reason, The Critique Of Practical Reason, Critique Of Judgement and others

"The Classic Collection of Immanuel Kant" is a comprehensive anthology that brings together the most influential works of one of the greatest philosophers in Western history. This collection features Kant's seminal texts, including "The Critique of Pure Reason," "The Critique of Practical Reason," "Critique of Judgment," and more, enhanced with captivating illustrations. In "The Critique of Pure Reason," Kant explores the boundaries of human knowledge and the nature of reality itself. His groundbreaking examination of metaphysics and epistemology has left an indelible mark on philosophy, challenging the way we perceive the world and our place in it. "The Critique of Practical Reason" delves into the realm of ethics and morality. Kant's exploration of the moral imperative and the concept of duty continues to shape discussions on ethics and the foundations of moral decision-making. "Critique of Judgment" delves into aesthetics and teleology, offering profound insights into the appreciation of beauty, the nature of art, and the philosophy of biology. Kant's thoughts on the judgment of taste and the teleological structure of the natural world have had a lasting impact on aesthetics and biology. Accompanied by evocative illustrations, "The Classic Collection of Immanuel Kant" not only presents these monumental philosophical treatises but also enhances the reader's engagement with the material. Kant's profound ideas and rigorous philosophy are beautifully complemented by the artwork, inviting readers to delve into the depths of his philosophical inquiries. This anthology pays homage to Immanuel Kant's enduring legacy as a philosopher who reshaped the landscape of human thought. Whether readers are drawn to metaphysics, ethics, aesthetics, or the fundamental questions of human existence, this collection offers a comprehensive and enriching exploration of Kant's profound philosophical contributions. Contents: The Critique Of Pure Reason The Critique Of Practical Reason Fundamental Principles Of The Metaphysic Of Morals The Metaphysical Elements Of Ethics The Injustice Of Counterfeiting Books Critique Of Judgement Perpetual Peace Prolegomena
